behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Evidence: behavior requires that speech-to-text output itself "preserves speaker labels" for attendee attribution. That depends on real model diarization quality, not just wiring. Lower-level miss-proof: a mocked STT response with perfect labels would always pass and hide real diarization/label-loss failures. Rejected higher level (Workflow): text does not require multiple agentic decisions in sequence; this can be validated in a single real STT+summary attribution check focused on label preservation.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real meeting audio sample with known speakers through the real STT API and summary step; assert transcript segments retain correct speaker labels and summary attributions map to the expected attendees.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets parsing logic on transcript text and output structure; failure is local extraction/mapping logic, with no required OS/network/subprocess or model-quality dependence. Rejected higher level (Integration): behavior text is about extraction in one parser boundary, not cross-module state handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser fixtures containing explicit assignments (e.g., "Alice will prepare Q2 budget") and assert extracted objects include correct assignee and task text fields.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Evidence: requirement is about final "live summary output" containing action items per assigned attendee, which depends on real LLM extraction/completeness decisions. Lower-level miss-proof: deterministic mocked summaries can always include all attendees and hide omission failures common in real model outputs. Rejected higher level (Workflow): behavior text does not require audio/transcription stages; it can be tested from transcript-to-summary with one real model decision boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a transcript with task assignments for multiple attendees to the real summarization model and assert each assigned attendee appears in at least one action item in structured output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: This is routing/mapping logic once decisions are already identified; the failure boundary is local schema-placement logic. Rejected higher level (Integration): wording "routes identified decisions" disqualifies dependence on upstream identification modules for this specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Pass a fixture with pre-identified decision entries into the routing function and assert output places them only under `decisions`, with narrative fields unchanged.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: Evidence: behavior checks whether key decisions from transcript are surfaced in the model-produced summary sectioning; this is model judgment/quality. Lower-level miss-proof: stubbing model output with pre-filled `decisions` will pass even when the real model buries decisions in narrative text. Rejected higher level (Workflow): text starts from transcript and only requires summary placement, so one real agentic summarization step is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Send transcripts containing explicit decision statements to the real summarizer and assert those decisions appear in the structured `decisions` section and are not only present in narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Evidence: behavior explicitly requires "splits ... into segments", "processes them sequentially", and "merging" into one coherent summary, which is multiple agentic steps with inter-step handoff. Lower-level miss-proof: a single-call agentic test or integration stubs can miss failures in cross-segment context carryover, ordering, and merge coherence that appear only across sequential real model calls. Rejected lower levels: Integration/System do not cover model-decision quality across multi-step sequence; Agentic single-step is insufficient for the stated sequential merge behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute meeting recording through the production segmentation path; verify segment-by-segment processing occurs in order and final merged summary is structurally valid and coherent across segment boundaries (no dropped/duplicated major items).

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Evidence: behavior is an end-to-end "full pipeline" over a "real meeting recording" spanning upload, transcription, summarization, and delivery; that includes multiple real agentic stages in sequence plus real infrastructure. Lower-level miss-proof: System-only infra checks or isolated agentic checks can pass while end-to-end orchestration between transcribe and summarize stages fails (handoff/schema/state issues) requiring full sequential run. No higher level exists beyond Workflow in this taxonomy.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an unattended end-to-end run with a real meeting audio file through production-like pipeline entrypoint and assert successful completion with delivered structured output containing required top-level sections and valid schema.
